https://www.shopify.com/blog/118363973-the-quick-and-easy-way-to-put-your-facebook-
ads-on-autopilot
1. Install Pixel on Shopify
• From your Shopify admin, go to Online Store > Preferences.
• Find the Facebook Pixel section, and then enter your Facebook Pixel ID.
• Click Save.
1. Create Product Catalog in Business Manager
• Your product feed must follow strict formatting rules and contain a set of
required fields to work properly. To get a better idea of how you should set
up your product feed, take a look at the example feeds created by Facebook’s
support team. There are multiple templates to download.
https://developers.facebook.com/docs/marketing-api/dynamic-product-
ads/product-catalog#feed-format
2. Create your Product Catalog in Facebook Business Manager
• Head to your Business Manager account. Click on Business Manager in the
top left of the navigation bar and then click ‘Product Catalogs’ under the
‘Assets’ header
• Click ‘Add New Product Catalog’ and select ‘Create New Product Catalog’
from the dropdown menu
• You’ll then be able to name your product feed, adjust your currency, and
choose an upload type for your feed. The two options are ‘Schedule
Recurring Uploads’ and ‘Single Upload’
• Choose single upload and upload your excel sheet
• Your product catalog will now be ready to go
3. Create your Dynamic Product Ad
• Click ‘Create Campaign’ and select ‘Promote a Product Catalog’ as your
objective
• In your ad set, you’ll be able to specifically filter which products you want
your ads to feature, also called a ‘Product Set’ by selecting the “+” button
under “Promoted Products”.
• After creating a product set, head down to the “Audience” section.
• Here, you’ll be able to select whether you’re hoping to target customers who
have viewed content on your website, added products to their cart, or
successfully purchased products. These custom audiences have thankfully
already been created for you by the Facebook Pixel which is a huge relief
since they’d be incredibly time-consuming (and headache-inducing) to set up
manually.
• When choosing an “Upsell” or “Cross-Sell” audience, you’ll also be able to
specify which product sets you want to promote to customers that have
already viewed other product sets. This is a nifty feature for turning your
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

one-time shoppers into repeat customers by showcasing products that they
could also be interested in or that work well with the things that they’ve
already purchased.
• Now that your ad set has been properly optimized for Dynamic Product Ads,
it’s time to create your ad. Go to the “Ads” tab and either create a new ad or
choose one that you’ve already created within that ad set.
